pi_items.metadata_value: NHTSA campaign numbers follow pattern YYV##### (e.g., 20V314000)

pi_items.metadata_value: Verifier checks pattern: ^\d{2}[A-Z]\d+$ which would match YYV##### or any YY[A-Z]#####
pi_items.verified_value: Pattern is overly permissive - allows any letter [A-Z] not just 'V', but all verified real NHTSA campaigns use 'V'
pi_items.source: Regex analysis from verifier.py line 148: nhtsa_campaign_pattern = re.compile(r'^\d{2}[A-Z]\d+$')

privileged_info_summary: CRITICAL FINDING: The task.json file is malformed and incomplete. It contains only 661 bytes and ends abruptly in the middle of a string without closing the JSON object or providing any metadata field. No metadata claims exist to verify. However, independent verification confirms: (1) NHTSA recall search database exists at nhtsa.gov/recalls; (2) All five specified vehicles (2019 Honda CR-V, 2020 Toyota Camry, 2021 Chevrolet Equinox, 2018 Ford F-150, 2022 Hyundai Tucson) exist and have real NHTSA recalls with documented campaign numbers; (3) NHTSA campaign numbers follow the format YYV##### (e.g., 20V314000, 21V215000), which matches the verifier's regex pattern. The verifier code itself appears sound, but it cannot function without a complete task.json and valid report data.
